page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors. It features 48 physical cores with 48 dedicated vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz, utilizing a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The memory subsystem provides 192.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled as a 2850 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. The networking baseline is 25 Gbps, and the instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a top-tier rating in PassMark memory score and database operations, alongside a top-tier throughput of 136.39 million operations per second in Redis SET operations. Gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. However, while LLM prompt processing for a small 135M model is top-tier at 4218.03 tokens per second, the text generation speed for the same model is poor at 31.56 tokens per second, indicating a performance tradeoff in sequential token generation.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high resource density, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local NVMe storage without the overhead of hyperthreading. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local SSD make it suitable for database operations, static web serving, caching layers, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing the ARM64 architecture with 48 dedicated physical cores on AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. The instance also demonstrates strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities. A notable tradeoff is present in LLM workloads, where prompt processing for small models is top-tier, but text generation speed is rated as poor. This hardware profile is suited for database workloads, caching systems, and web serving applications requiring high local storage performance and balanced memory-to-CPU resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 physical cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and Redis performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 48 cores, 192 GB RAM, and a 2850 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for database and web workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier database and memory benchmark performance.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors with 48 dedicated physical cores.
Includes 192.0 GB of RAM and a 2850 GB local NVMe SSD instance store.
Delivers top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks.
Provides a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
Exhibits a performance tradeoff with poor LLM text generation despite top-tier prompt processing.
Suited for database applications, static web serving, and caching workloads.
